[Skip to main content](https://www.icoterminals.com/en#main-content)

# Discover our terminals

[Our terminals](https://www.icoterminals.com/en/terminals)

### Ship Handling

### Vehicle Processing

### Terminal Handling

### Breakbulk & Projects

### Agency & Logistics

![](https://www.icoterminals.com/sites/default/files/styles/pg_text_media/public/2023-01/hoofdkantoor.jpg?itok=OquKiLs0)

## International car operators

ICO is the global market leader in the port handling and storage of roll-on/roll-off cargo on its various deepsea terminals. In addition to loading and unloading new vehicles, construction materials, breakbulk, heavy lifts and any other RoRo cargo, we offer vehicle processing, shipping agency and various services related to Customs and fiscal representation, as well as the full door-to-door logistics from factory up to delivery to the client / dealer.

ICO is a 100% subsidiary of Nippon Yusen Kaisha (NYK), one of world's most important transport companies. We are established in Belgium, in the heart of the European automotive industry. Via the ports of Antwerp and Zeebrugge we handle more than 2 million cars per year for all the major car manufacturers and shipping companies. World's main car brands from Europe and Japan have included ICO in their logistic supply chain.

The geographical location of the terminals in Zeebrugge and Antwerp, a perfect mix of import and export volumes, the intra-European and global network of destinations connected to ICO, combined with state-of-the-art terminals and well-skilled workers, are the strategic factors of ICO's success.

## Our corporate values

Since the satisfaction of the customer is our first concern, we have based our services on **3 values**. We are strongly committed to those every day in all our departments.

### INTEGRITY

Be respectful and nice for customers and colleagues. Stay warm, friendly, polite and caring.

### INNOVATION

Always think of new ideas for improvement, even when the circumstances look satisfactory. Be open for improvement.

### INTENSITY

Work on every task until it is completed. Do not give up. Overcome challenges. Stay motivated.

## International network

ICO serves an extensive network of deep-sea shipping companies serving destinations worldwide to and from all continents. Our terminals in Zeebrugge and Antwerp are an international hub for roll-on/roll-off goods handling.

Inland distribution


Shortsea distribution


Deepsea connections


![](https://www.icoterminals.com/sites/default/files/2023-04/inland_distribution_2.png)

![](https://www.icoterminals.com/sites/default/files/2023-02/shortsea_connections.png)

![](https://www.icoterminals.com/sites/default/files/2023-02/deepsea_connections.png)

### 100% subsidiary of NYK

### Located in Belgium   375ha terminal in Zeebrugge & Antwerp

### 1250 staff   (incl. dockworkers)

### 2,4 million cars in 2022

### Ship handling   2500 vessels/year

### Connected to 191 ports worldwide   49 in EU - 142 rest of world

### 11 windmills (44 Mw)   308 charging points

### 5 PDI centers

![](https://www.icoterminals.com/sites/default/files/styles/pg_text_media/public/2023-04/ico_03-10-2022_3469.jpg?itok=w05RJ5D6)

## CLIMATE neutral

ICO endorses the transition to a climate neutral society. We organize our activities with respect for the environment and the people who give the best of themselves here every day. We are investing in renewable energy to limit our CO2 emissions, we are fully committed to smart energy management of our buildings and we are reducing our ecological footprint even more with numerous sustainability initiatives. Reusing rainwater, recycling materials, sensitizing our employees to adapted (driving) behaviour... We like to shift up a gear for the environment.

[Read more](https://www.icoterminals.com/en/sustainability "Sustainability")

## Do you have a QUESTION?

Would you like more information about our activities? Are you curious what we can do for you? Contact us. We are happy to help you.

[Contact us](https://www.icoterminals.com/en/contact)

[Close](https://www.icoterminals.com/en#)

web.markdown:
1. [TOP](https://www.nyk.com/english/)
2. [News Room](https://www.nyk.com/english/news/)
3. [News Releases : 2025](https://www.nyk.com/english/news/2025/)
4. Europe's First Fully Automated Multilevel Parking System for Finished Vehicles to Be Constructed at the Port of Zeebrugge, Belgium

Nov. 04, 2025

_Press Release_

# Europe's First Fully Automated Multilevel Parking System for Finished Vehicles to Be Constructed at the Port of Zeebrugge, Belgium

## Enhancing efficiency and reducing Green House Gas (GHG) emissions through workforce optimization

International Car Operators N.V. (“ICO”),\* a wholly owned subsidiary of the NYK Group, has on September 15th obtained formal approval from the Belgian government for the construction of a fully automated multi-level parking facility at its finished vehicle terminal in the port of Zeebrugge, Belgium. This facility will be the first of its kind at a European finished vehicle terminal.

The project aims to enhance operational efficiency and cut GHG emissions, thereby contributing to the development of the automotive supply chain in Europe in the most environmentally friendly way.

ICO’s current vehicle terminal is among the largest in the world and boasts the largest cargo handling capacity in Europe. The facility presently has flat parking surfaces spanning 250 hectares with a combined capacity of approximately 75,000 vehicles.

The construction of this new, automated multi-level parking system on a 2.5-hectare footprint will add approximately 10,000 parking spaces. This expansion will enable ICO to increase its annual vehicle handling capacity from 2.5 million to 3 million units, positioning the facility to accommodate the growth of the European automotive market.

Additionally, the multilevel parking system offers enhanced efficiency and flexibility. Rather than requiring drivers to manually maneuver vehicles, the automated system enables drivers to simply activate an entrance device, after which the technology seamlessly relocates vehicles within the multi-level garage. This automation minimizes risks of human error and bolsters the safety of cargo operations.

In addition, all cargo-handling machinery in the new facility will be powered by electricity generated from wind turbines\*\* installed at the terminal. Furthermore, the terminal’s close proximity to ship-berthing positions reduces the driving distance between the parking facility and the vessels, contributing to a significant reduction in GHG emissions.

![](https://www.nyk.com/english/news/2025/__icsFiles/afieldfile/2025/10/31/ico_1.png)

Concept mage of the automatic multi-level parking system

\*International Car Operators N.V. (ICO)

A Belgium-based wholly owned subsidiary of the NYK Group, ICO operates dedicated finished vehicle terminals at the ports of Zeebrugge and Antwerp. These ports serve as gateway ports for the import and export of finished vehicles in Europe. In addition to cargo handling services, ICO provides value-added services such as pre-delivery inspections and accessory installations.

Headquarters: Zeebrugge, Belgium

Website: [http://www.icoterminals.com/en](http://www.icoterminals.com/en)

\\*\\* Wind power generation

At ICO's terminal, 11 wind turbines generate electricity that is planned to be used to power the fully automated multi-level parking garage.

Press release: [Wind power generation begins at Belgium's finished vehicle terminal \| Nippon Yusen Kabushiki Kaisha](https://www.nyk.com/news/2021/20210215_01.html)

[News Releases : 2025](https://www.nyk.com/english/news/2025/)

## About International Car Operators

ICO is a leading global company specializing in the handling and storage of roll-on/roll-off cargo, including new cars, construction materials, and heavy lifts. They provide comprehensive logistics solutions, including vehicle processing, ship agency services, and customs handling, primarily serving major automotive brands and RoRo shipping lines. With terminals located in Antwerp and Zeebrugge, ICO processes over 2.5 million vehicles annually and connects with 191 ports worldwide. The company is committed to sustainability and innovation, focusing on customer satisfaction and environmental responsibility.Explore more

# Jobs

ICO is committed to employee well-being. We are a people-driven company: our people are central and are given every opportunity to develop a great career. To this end, we offer them tailor-made training, daily coaching, sponsorship… In short, everyone is given every opportunity to grow optimally in line with his or her capabilities.

[View all vacancies](https://www.icoterminals.com/en/jobs#vacatures)

### SELECTION PROCEDURE

Everything starts with selecting the right people for the right position. We do this through a transparent selection and recruitment policy. When an applicant is found to be competent, he/she can start.

### REWARD & PROMOTION

We reward and promote our employees based on their performance. We pay a lot of attention to consultation, continuous evaluation and open two-way communication.

### OPTIMAL WORKING ENVIRONMENT

In everything we do, in every function, safety and innovation are always at the forefront. We want our people to be able to perform their work in the best conditions, without risk. Maximum performance is always better in an optimal working environment.

![](https://www.icoterminals.com/sites/default/files/styles/pg_text_media/public/2025-09/foto_website.jpg?itok=xIx6gCtd)

## See what our people think of their job:

[![Werken bij ICO](https://www.icoterminals.com/sites/default/files/styles/pg_media/public/2023-06/ico1_0.png?itok=r3iNb_JQ)](https://www.youtube.com/embed/FtqCq9Ie2gg)

## Our vacancies

When you apply for a job with us, you will receive an email from us with your login details. Your application will be assessed on the basis of your data by the manager of the department. All further communication takes place via your personal application page. So keep an eye on these.

web.title: [PDF] ICO Terminals - Intris
web.description: In the four Vehicle Processing Centres the new cars are made fully ready for delivery or shipment, including predelivery inspection, fitting of accessories and ...
web.markdown:
## CASE

# ICO Terminals

* * *

### Raymond Troch, manager at ICO Terminals:

# “At Intris you keep that feeling of customer contact”

ICO nv is a world leader in ro/ro handling and storage. Apart from loading and unloading new cars, construction equipment and heavy lifts, the company offers a whole series of additional services: vehicle processing, ship’s agent, full customs formalities ... in fact a complete door-to-door service, from factory to end delivery to the customer.

“We offer our automotive customers an all-inclusive package,” explains All together these terminals have a car capacity of 126,000 units. In manager Raymond Troch. “Loading and unloading cars, handling the the four Vehicle Processing Centres the new cars are made fully cars in our VPC, storage in the warehouse, customs clearance and so ready for delivery or shipment, including predelivery inspection, fitting on.” ICO has a presence in Antwerp with a concession in the Vrasene of accessories and repairs. ICO naturally carries out these logistics terminal (the largest ro/ro terminal in the port, on the Left bank area), services for its own customers, but it also offers its know-how and and in Zeebrugge it operates three terminals in the inner port: Northern expertise to third parties. This expertise also covers all aspects of Mooring Terminal, Hansa Terminal and Bastenaken Terminal. customs clearance: import and export facilities, tax representation, transit documents, depot management etc.

_“We opted for Intris because their software complied fully with our vision and our way of thinking.“_

#### CaseICO Terminals

* * *

Tris Customs

“We’ve been working with this software package for ten years or so now,”
says Troch. “We opted for Intris because they had the most experience
with the practical aspects of customs clearance, combined with IT
know-how. In fact they were the only ones who could immediately
answer our questions, and their software complied fully with our vision
and our way of thinking. Their external consultant Michel Van Giel also
offers great added value.”

”We can see at a glance how our customers have
performed.”

As a consequence of the previous history of ICO (formed by merger of
ZSB/CTO in Zeebrugge and the car division of Hessenatie in Antwerp)
the terminals in Zeebrugge and Antwerp each had their own operating
system which was suddenly replaced in 2012 with a single, joint system
for both ports.

“This was our big bang moment, but we received a huge amount
of support for it from Intris, as they integrated our operating system
seamlessly with their Customs package. Import duties and VAT were
also involved, so it was extremely important for nothing to go wrong
during the switch-over. We had people from Intris helping us on the spot
during the transition weekend, and everything went smoothly.”

New Responsibilities

For its existing terminal customers ICO offers customs-related
services, for example, activities that come under the heading of actively
adding value. In addition goods are regularly stored under customs
supervision, with import declarations or transit declarations being made
subsequently.

Under the terms of the new EU customs legislation the terminal bears
joint responsibility for customs declarations. But according to Troch
this does not pose any problems for the day-to-day activities: “Michel
Van Giel ensured that we were well prepared for this. We applied for
our Temporary Storage permit in good time, and our people on the quay
were given customs training so that they knew which documents were
needed for incoming goods. We now have everything fully under control:
nothing leaves here without valid customs documentation.”

“When we tell customers that we also offer this added value, it’s
partly thanks to Intris,” Troch emphasises. ICO also uses the Customs
Communication Tool for direct communication with the Customs
service, so that all aspects of the declarations are fully managed and
followed up.

Case

“This module offers more functions than we actually use,” Torch admits.
“But it works fine for us, and we’re very satisfied with the reporting: every
month we can see at a glance how our customers have performed, and
we can immediately provide our principals with a monthly report. Once
we have switched to the new TRIS solution on Oracle we actually will
use all the functions, so this will offer added value for the customers.”

“When we tell customers that we also offer this added
value, it’s partly thanks to Intris”

Tris Forwarding

Greater productivity with the same number of people, more control and
better risk analysis: these are the main benefits that Raymond Troch
sees in the TRIS solutions. But he considers the way in which Intris treats
its customers to be just as important, with regular review meetings to
check that everything is running as it should, with both CEOs attending.
“At Intris you keep that feeling of customer contact,” he concludes.

Advantages

* * *

Put logic in your logistics

We are the only software supplier to have AEO certification for direct
electronic data exchange with the European Customs authorities.

A logistics chain is only as strong as its weakest link, and so it is essential for the underlying software to also be 100% reliable. Intris gives you that
guarantee: we are the only software supplier to have AEO certification for direct electronic data exchange with the European Customs authorities.

TRIS Forwarding

TRIS Customs

With TRIS Warehouse Management Solution you can manage all your
operational steps from A to Z: planning, goods in, transfers, goods
out etc. All combined with powerful file management: from offer to
invoicing, including document management for the warehousing and
transport activities.
